crime.value: Fatal gunfight/mutual killing between Wolf Tone Maloney and Thomas Grimthorpe (Tattooed Tom)
crime.quote: Shots were exchanged, and both were badly wounded, only surviving a few minutes.

victim.value: Wolf Tone Maloney, former murderer and government informant with many enemies from his criminal past
victim.quote: On Friday evening he encountered an old enemy, Thomas Grimthorpe, commonly known as Tattooed Tom

culprit_and_motive.value: Thomas Grimthorpe killed Maloney in revenge as an 'old enemy', likely related to Maloney's past betrayals
culprit_and_motive.quote: he encountered an old enemy, Thomas Grimthorpe

resolution.value: Natural conclusion through mutual destruction - both men died in the gunfight, ending the conflict
resolution.quote: both were badly wounded, only surviving a few minutes

investigator.value: Prison doctor who befriended Maloney and heard his confession before death
investigator.quote: Give the doc my share, for he's as good

red_herring.value: Initial misdirection suggests Maloney is reformed citizen and bar owner, concealing his true identity as mass murderer
red_herring.quote: W. T. Maloney, a well-know citizen of New Montrose, and proprietor of the Yellow Boy gambling saloon

clue_fairness.value: All details of Maloney's criminal past are revealed through his own confession to the doctor-narrator
clue_fairness.quote: That man's story's worth listening to if you could get him to tell it

social_world.value: Prison hierarchy with doctor, warders, and numbered convicts; colonial Australian frontier justice and vigilante law
social_world.quote: Number 481 is no better, doctor, said the head-warder, in a slightly reproachful accent

reveal_mechanic.value: Truth revealed through dying confession and corroborated by newspaper account of the fatal gunfight
reveal_mechanic.quote: I cannot conclude without borrowing the account of the fatal quarrel which appeared in the column

media_and_audience.value: Newspaper transforms wholesale murderer into respectable citizen, sanitizing his criminal history for public consumption
media_and_audience.quote: W. T. Maloney, a well-know citizen of New Montrose, and proprietor of the Yellow Boy gambling saloon

investigator_wound.value: Prison doctor's professional isolation and curiosity about his patients' hidden histories
investigator_wound.quote: coming back every night as tired as a dog, and you talk about finding an excuse for doing more work

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle my friend the murderer
